title: Ranunculus prasinaster
intro: Ranunculus prasinaster, also known as Green Buttercup, is a perennial plant species within the Ranunculus genus, characterized by its bright green, rounded flowers and lobed leaves. The plant is rich in bioactive constituents, including ranunculin, protoanemonin, and isorhapontigenin, which possess anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. Traditional herbal medicine has utilized the Green Buttercup for its potential health benefits, including treating wounds, infections, and respiratory issues, as well as providing relief from pain and inflammation. The most common herbal preparations made from Ranunculus prasinaster include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are often used to create topical creams and ointments for skin and wound care.
